description_long: This object is a rustic wooden fence composed of two separate sections, each constructed from uneven, horizontally aligned rails attached to vertical posts. The rails are irregularly shaped, giving the fence a handcrafted, natural appearance, and are fastened to the posts with visible bolts or fasteners. The wood is a medium brown color, and the surface appears matte with a slightly rough texture, typical of untreated or lightly finished timber. There are no decorative elements or post caps, and the top edge of the fence is uneven, following the natural contours of the rails. The fence is low in height, suitable for marking boundaries in parks, gardens, or along sidewalks without obstructing views. The open gaps between the rails and the relatively short stature make it easy to see and potentially step over.
description: A rustic, low wooden fence with irregular horizontal rails and a natural, handcrafted look. The fence is brown, matte, and lacks decorative elements or post caps.
description_view_0: Front view showing two sections of the fence with uneven horizontal rails and visible fasteners.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the fence and the separation between the two sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same irregular rails and posts, with the rails' unevenness clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the gap between the two fence sections.
